tool.name: Genetic Clustering
tool.description: with LEA (sNMF) for VCF or SSR data
tool.command:
mkdir -p outputs &&
#if str($input_format) == "vcf": cp '$input_data' ./input_data.vcf && #set $input_path = './input_data.vcf' #else: cp '$input_data' ./input_data.txt && #set $input_path = './input_data.txt' #end if
Rscript '$__tool_directory__/LEA_snmf.R' --input '$input_path' --format '$input_format' #if str($k_mode.k_mode_sel) == "fixed": --k-fixed '$k_mode.k_fixed' #else: --k-min '$k_mode.k_min' --k-max '$k_mode.k_max' --fst-check '$k_mode.fst_check' #end if

param.name: input_data
param.label: Upload your genetic data
param.help: File containing SNP data in VCF format or SSR/microsatellite data in tabular format (see help section).

param.name: k_fixed
param.label: Select the value of K
param.help: Number of clusters to be used for analysis.

param.name: k_min
param.label: Minimum K
param.help: Lower limit of the range of K tested.

param.name: k_max
param.label: Maximum K
param.help: Upper limit. Increase if the selected K=K max.

param.name: fst_check
param.label: Validate K with Fst differentiation test
param.help: If enabled, the best K from cross-entropy is validated using pairwise Fst (genet.dist, hierfstat) with a permutation test (999 permutations). If Fst between clusters is not significant, K is decremented by 1 and re-tested. Only pure individuals (ancestry proportion >= 80%) are used for this test. Warning: this step can be slow on large datasets.

tool.help:
Usage =====
As input, the user provides genetic data in VCF format for SNPs (Danecek et al. 2011) or in a tabular format for SSRs (see format below).
This tool runs the sNMF algorithm from the R package LEA to estimate individual ancestry proportions and infer population structure. It automatically converts input data to the required .geno format and evaluates a range of ancestral populations (K) from K_min to K_max.
To ensure the stability of the results, the tool performs a defined number of repetitions (default: 10) for each value of K. The estimation also uses a regularization parameter (alpha), where a value of 100 is recommended for datasets with fewer than 10,000 SNPs, while a value of 10 is more suitable for larger datasets.
The tool selects the best K using the cross-entropy criterion (lowest mean = best fit). Optionally, it can validate the chosen K via a pairwise Fst​ permutation test. In this validation step, only "pure" individuals (ancestry proportion ≥ 80%) are used to ensure clusters are well-differentiated; if the minimum Fst​ is not significant (p≥0.05), K is decremented and re-tested.
The tool generates two figures : the cross-entropy plot and a STRUCTURE-style barplot, as well as a tabular file summarizing the ancestry and status (Pure or Admixed) of each individual.
Examples files ==============
**SSR Genotype file (tab):**
::
Ind Pop Loc1 Loc2 Loc3 ind1 pop1 120/124 200/202 150/150 ind2 pop1 120/120 202/204 150/152 ind3 pop2 124/126 200/200 152/152
**Ancestry summary output table:**
+-----------+-----+------------------+---------+ | Individu | Pop | Appartenance_max | Statut | +===========+=====+==================+=========+ | Sample_01 | 1 | 0.923 | Pure | +-----------+-----+------------------+---------+ | Sample_02 | 2 | 0.871 | Pure | +-----------+-----+------------------+---------+ | Sample_03 | 1 | 0.612 | Admixed | +-----------+-----+------------------+---------+
Note: The Pop column indicates the majority cluster assigned by the algorithm. Individuals are classified as Admixed if their majority ancestry proportion is below 80%.
See also examples of VCF formats in : https://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/VCFv4.5.pdf

citations.citation: @Book{, author = {Hadley Wickham}, title = {ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis}, publisher = {Springer-Verlag New York}, year = {2016}, isbn = {978-3-319-24277-4}, url = {https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org}, }
